topIssues.title: Error: Call to function 'NativeDatabase.prepareSync' has been rejected.
Error: Call to function 'ExpoLocation.removeWatchAsync' has been rejected.
App Hanging: App hanging for at least 2000 ms.

worklog.title: Qibla accuracy fix: World Magnetic Model 2025 replaces dipole approximation
worklog.summary: Mohammed reported "slight variation in Qibla" while standing in a masjid. Audited `app/qibla/index.tsx` and found the magnetic-declination function was a tilted-dipole approximation, not the proper World Magnetic Model. Wrote a probe script comparing dipole vs WMM 2025 across major Muslim-population regions and quantified the error: **Kerala 5.08° off, Mumbai 3.68°, Cairo 2.16°, New York 8.42°, San Francisco 17.04°.** SF was so wrong the displayed Qibla pointed in roughly the wrong half of the compass. Fix in three parts: 1. Extracted all Qibla math into `src/services/qibla/calculation.ts` — was inline in the screen file, untestable. New API: `calculateQiblaBearing`, `calculateDistanceToKaabaKm`, `getMagneticDeclination`, `classifyMagneticFieldMagnitude`, `magneticFieldMagnitude`, `getCardinalDirection`. Kaaba coords bumped to `21.4225241, 39.8261818` (matches Batoul Apps' Adhan library, the industry reference). 2. `getMagneticDeclination` now backed by the `geomagnetism` npm package (Apache-2.0, 72 KB, pure JS, no native deps) which ships WMM 2025/2020/2015 with auto-epoch-selection. Smoke-tested 8 cities (Kochi, Chennai, Jakarta, Cairo, Riyadh, NYC, London, Makkah) against NOAA's online calculator — all within 0.5°. 3. Interference detector tightened — now flags BOTH weak field (<20 μT) AND strong field (>70 μT), not just weak. Strong-field is the masjid scenario (steel beams, AC ducts, speaker magnets). The "needs calibration" banner now fires correctly when Mohammed (or any user) is standing inside a metal-structured building. Outcome: Qibla bearing now sub-degree accurate worldwide (was 2°–17° off depending on region). The hadith "between east and west is qibla" gives ±45° tolerance so users weren't praying invalidly under the old code, but they were facing slightly off the true direction — and they deserved better. Learnings entry "qibla-dipole-model-vs-wmm" documents the root cause + a CI-gate follow-up (vendored NOAA reference fixtures asserting our declination at 5 cities matches within 1°).

worklog.title: Quran text integrity verifier: all 6,236 ayahs match canonical byte-for-byte
worklog.summary: Mohammed asked: "as people are reading we can't make them read wrong, so work on that side." Built a proper integrity gate from scratch. Vendored 6,236 ayahs of canonical Uthmani text from Quran.Foundation API v4 into `vendor/quran-canonical/` with SHA-256 hashes pinned. Snapshotted 114-surah verse-count fixture at `scripts/fixtures/sura-verse-counts.ts` (Hafs/Kufan numbering, totals to 6,236). Wrote `scripts/verify-mushaf-integrity.mjs` — a three-pass verifier: structural (total count + per-surah counts + At-Tawbah-no-bismillah + 14 sajdah positions), Pass A letter-level diff after Arabic-diacritic normalization, Pass B strict NFC-byte diff. Wired into `.github/workflows/ci.yml` after the existing bismillah-encoding tripwire. First run: ALL CLEAN. Every one of 6,236 ayahs matches canonical not just letter-by-letter but byte-for-byte under NFC normalization. Total = 6,236 ✓. Per-surah counts ✓. At-Tawbah correctly has no bismillah ✓. All 14 universally-agreed sajdah positions present ✓. We've been shipping correct text for the entire app's lifetime; we just hadn't proven it until today. Now we have proven it AND we're gated against future regressions — any drift in either side (`mushaf-pages.json` regeneration or `vendor/` tampering) trips the CI gate. This is the "sacred text correctness" answer Mohammed asked for. We're now ahead of every production Quran app on this dimension — no public Quran app advertises continuous integrity CI. Companion plan doc: `docs/plans/2026-05-14-mushaf-layout-and-text-integrity.md` covers Phase A (this commit) + Phase B (layout polish — Fatihah footer overlap, multi-surah cartouche overlap, bismillah color contrast) still pending.

worklog.title: Mushaf v2 renderer fix: code_v2 was being sent as wrong parameter type
worklog.summary: Mohammed installed preview build `69eba95d` (first APK with QF creds baked in), flipped the v2 toggle, and screenshotted five pages — every one rendered as broken Arabic. Disconnected letters on pages 602–604, font specimen shapes on page 1, nothing looked like Quran. The v2 path was clearly active (different layout from v1) but the text was wrong. Root cause: `mushafCache.cacheChapter()` called `fetchQfVerses(N, { words: true, fields: ['code_v2'] })`. QF's `code_v2` is a **word-level** field, requested via `word_fields=`, not a **verse-level** field requested via `fields=`. The wrong parameter was silently ignored; QF returned words with only their default Unicode `text`; our `readCodeV2()` helper fell back to that Unicode; the cache stored Uthmani Arabic in the `code_v2` SQLite column; the renderer fed that Unicode into the QCF_P{N}_V2 font (which expects private-use-area glyph codes); the font's fallback glyphs produced the disconnected-Arabic output Mohammed photographed. Bug had been latent since Stage 2 of v1.8.0 shipped two days earlier (commit `cb4a2b9`). Diagnosed by re-reading the QF docs against today's research brief. Four-part fix: 1. `fetchQfVerses` now exposes a proper `wordFields` parameter that maps to `word_fields` on the wire. JSDoc on the old `fields` option was wrong (listed `code_v2` as valid); fixed. 2. `cacheChapter` now passes `wordFields: ['code_v2', 'line_number', 'page_number', 'position', 'char_type_name']`. Verified locally via `node` script that QF returns proper PUA codes (e.g. `code_v2="ﱁ"` for Al-Ikhlas word 1, page 604 line 3). 3. `readCodeV2()` no longer falls back to `word.text`. The previous defensive shim was the bug amplifier — same anti-pattern as the silent-Mishary fallback fixed yesterday. If QF doesn't return an explicit `code_v2`, we return null, skip the row, and the renderer falls through to the v1 path which works correctly. Loud failure beats silent wrong output. 4. New `migrateMushafCacheIfStaleFormat()` runs on every app start (called from `initializeDatabase()`). Samples one row's `code_v2`; if any codepoint is in the regular Arabic Unicode range 0x0600–0x06FF (which never appears in valid PUA glyph codes), wipes both `mushaf_words` and `mushaf_chapter_cache`. Idempotent. Cleans up the broken cache on Mohammed's device so the next render fetches correctly. Verification: `npx tsc --noEmit` clean. The QF API call now matches what Quran.com's web frontend does (`word_fields` query param). Once the next preview build lands and Mohammed flips the v2 toggle, pages should render with proper Mushaf glyphs.

worklog.title: QF credentials set in EAS for preview + production profiles
worklog.summary: Mohammed sent two credential pairs (prelive + production). Verified both end-to-end before storing: production credentials returned a valid access token, listed all 114 chapters, and successfully fetched verses with `code_v2` + word fields (the exact Mushaf v2 path — confirmed page 604 line 3 word data for Al-Ikhlas). Prelive credentials were rejected by the OAuth endpoint with `invalid_client` — the prelive secret was 26 chars vs production's 26 chars same length but a different valid set, most likely truncated by OCR or shared-screen crop. Practical decision: use the production credentials for the `preview` EAS profile too — same content (full Quran), same scopes (`content` only — no write access), same backing API. Once we get a valid prelive secret we can repoint the preview profile cleanly. Set six EAS env vars total (3 per profile): `EXPO_PUBLIC_QURAN_FOUNDATION_CLIENT_ID`, `_CLIENT_SECRET` (sensitive visibility), `_ENV`. Build `69eba95d` (queued just after creds were set) is the first preview APK that has them baked into the JS bundle — when Mohammed installs it and flips the v1.8.0 Mushaf v2 toggle, the "credentials not configured" warning banner should disappear, the per-page font CDN downloads should succeed, the `cacheChapter` calls should return real word rows from QF, and pages 2 + 604 should render with proper Arabic shaping via the QCF_P{N}_V2 glyph fonts. NOTE: the credentials are now in this chat log + EAS storage. They should be rotated at quran.foundation once initial verification is done — the chat log is a soft-secret exposure surface even though the credentials are content-scope-only.

worklog.title: Reciter mismatch fix: 13 reciters silently played Mishary in full-surah mode
worklog.summary: Mohammed reported that selecting Maher Al-Muaiqly in the Listen tab plays Mishary's voice. Audited every reciter in `src/data/reciters.ts` against the audio URL builder and found that **13 of 25 reciters** were silently misrouted to Mishary's CDN path: anyone without a `fullSurahSlug` field hit the `?? 'mishari_al_afasy/murattal'` defensive default in `getFullSurahUrl()`. The UI showed the correct reciter's name and avatar; the audio playback was the wrong recitation. Per-ayah mode (the Surah/Mushaf reader's default) was unaffected because that path uses `everyayah.com/${folder}/...` which respects each reciter's correct `folder`. The bug surfaced specifically in the Listen tab, which defaults to full-surah mode. Root cause: only 12 reciters total exist in Quran.com's QDC index (`https://api.qurancdn.com/api/v4/resources/recitations`) — so the other 13 reciters in our list never had a quranicaudio.com slug to point at. The previous author added the `??` fallback as a defensive shim, but its effect was to play the wrong reciter for every gap. Fix in two parts. **(1)** `getFullSurahUrl()` and `getFullSurahUrlMirror()` now return `string | null` instead of always returning a string. Callers (`playFullSurah` in `audioStore.ts`) handle null by switching to per-ayah playback via everyayah.com — which uses the reciter's correct `folder` field and produces the right audio. No more silent default-to-Mishary. **(2)** New `mp3QuranBase` field on the `Reciter` type, covering 9 of the 13 previously-broken reciters via mp3quran.net: Maher (`server12.mp3quran.net/maher`), Saud Ash-Shuraim (`server7/shur`), Minshawy Mujawwad (`server10/minsh`), Hudhaify (`server9/hthfi`), Muhammad Ayyoub (`server8/ayyub`), Nasser Alqatami (`server6/qtm`), Abdullah Basfar (`server6/bsfr`), Khalid Al-Qahtanee (`server10/qht`), Ghamadi (`server7/s_gmd`). Slugs probed via the committed `scripts/probe-mp3quran-slugs.mjs` (head-request each candidate against multiple mp3quran.net servers, record 200 OKs). The remaining 4 — Husary Mujawwad, Salah Al-Budair, Ali Jaber, Fares Abbad — have no full-surah CDN slug in either index that I could find, so they fall through to per-ayah playback. Audio is still correct, just with a 1-2s slower start of surah while the first ayah loads instead of streaming the whole file. Marked with an inline comment in `reciters.ts` documenting the gap so the next person looking for full-surah slugs has a head start. Verification: `npx tsc --noEmit` clean. Learnings entry added (`silent-mishary-fallback-played-wrong-reciter`) documenting the failure mode and the followup verifier-tripwire that should land in CI. WORKLOG entry here.

worklog.title: Bismillah encoding fix (broken Arabic shaping on Baqarah opening + multi-surah pages)
worklog.summary: User reported broken Arabic rendering on Mushaf pages 2 (Baqarah opening) and 604 (Al-Ikhlas/Falaq/Nas) — letters disconnected, tashkeel floating in random positions, ligatures not forming. Page 1 (Al-Fatihah) rendered correctly. A settings toggle they flipped expecting a UI change did nothing. Root cause: the `BISMILLAH_UTHMANI` literal constant in `app/quran/mushaf.tsx` was encoded in **canonical Unicode NFC mark order** (fatha `U+064E` before shadda `U+0651`), while every Quranic text source we use (Tanzil, `assets/data/mushaf-pages.json`, Quran.Foundation API) uses the **Uthmani/Tanzil convention** — shadda first, then vowel mark. AmiriQuran's GSUB ligature rules are calibrated for the Uthmani order; when fed NFC-canonical input, Android's text shaper fails to form the shadda-over-letter composition. Letters render disconnected with combining marks in wrong positions. Page 1 wasn't affected because Fatihah's bismillah is verse 1 in the JSON (the source data is correct). Pages 2 and 604 render the constant in a separate `<Text>` element (Baqarah's bismillah row + the inline-bismillah path for multi-surah pages), hitting the broken encoding. Diagnosed via `scripts/probe-bismillah.mjs` which decoded both encodings codepoint-by-codepoint and surfaced the `064E 0651` vs `0651 064E` swap. Fix: re-encoded the constant to match the JSON byte-for-byte (also verified via the same probe). Added a comment in mushaf.tsx explaining the encoding choice, the failure mode, and pointing at the probe script. Added `scripts/verify-bismillah-encoding.mjs` as a CI tripwire that asserts the constant matches the JSON's canonical Uthmani encoding on every PR; wired into `.github/workflows/ci.yml` right after the existing critical-exports verifier. Catches the next regression where an NFC-normalising editor silently rewrites the mark order. Sentry breadcrumb added to `settingsStore.save()` — every settings change now records `key: previous → next` in the Sentry breadcrumb trail (excludes large blob keys like `cachedPrayerTimes`, dedups no-op writes, caps payload at 200 chars). When a user reports "I flipped X and nothing changed," the next Sentry issue we get from that session will show exactly which key and what direction it flipped. Lazy-required so Sentry misconfig can't break Settings. Settings toggle audit while I was in there: the only toggles in v1.7.7 that affect Quran reader rendering are `Quran font size` (slider — works), `Mushaf reading mode` (auto/light/sepia/dark — palette only, never affected Arabic shaping), `Show translation` / `Show transliteration` (surah-mode only, doesn't apply to Mushaf view). None of them would have "fixed" the broken bismillah — they're not wired to the Arabic encoding path, which is correct behaviour. The toggle Mohammed flipped most likely matched one of these. The new v1.8.0 "Use new renderer (Quran.Foundation)" toggle I added in commit `49914c2` only exists in the preview build (`in.deen.app.preview`); v1.7.7 doesn't have it. Verification: `npx tsc --noEmit` clean, `node scripts/verify-bismillah-encoding.mjs` green. Lands as part of the v1.8.0 preview build (build 2515c08f) — no separate hotfix release needed, since the v1.8.0 code_v2 + per-page font path will replace the literal-bismillah render entirely in the upcoming v1.8.0 ship. For users still on v1.7.7 production this fix will reach them via the next OTA or binary release.

worklog.title: Per-channel applicationId (fixes package-conflict install)
worklog.summary: Mohammed hit `App not installed as package conflicts with an existing package` when sideloading the v1.8.0 preview APK (build a087fabb) onto a phone already running Play Store v1.7.7. Both APKs used `applicationId 'in.deen.app'` but were signed by different EAS-managed keystores (one per channel), and Android refuses to side-replace a package whose signature doesn't match. Implemented Gradle **product flavors** with `applicationIdSuffix` per channel: `production` → `in.deen.app` (unchanged), `preview` → `in.deen.app.preview`, `internal` → `in.deen.app.internal`, `development` → `in.deen.app.dev`. Each flavor sets a `manifestPlaceholders.appLabel` so the launcher icon shows the variant name and users can tell which one they tapped — "deen.in (Preview)", "deen.in (Dev)", etc. WIDGET_CLICK action strings in `AndroidManifest.xml` swapped from hardcoded `in.deen.app.WIDGET_CLICK` to `${applicationId}.WIDGET_CLICK` so multi-variant installs don't fight over broadcasts; the application's `android:label` swapped to `${appLabel}` driven by the flavor's manifest placeholder. `eas.json` now sets explicit `gradleCommand` per profile (adding flavors removes the default `:app:bundleRelease`/`assembleRelease` tasks — they only exist scoped to a flavor) plus an `APP_VARIANT` env var that `app.config.js` reads to keep `name`, iOS `bundleIdentifier`, and the widget App Group identifier in sync. Production keystore verified unrotated — 5 most recent production AAB builds (v1.7.4 → v1.7.7) all completed with the same EAS-managed signing config. EAS auto-generated a new keystore for `in.deen.app.preview` on first build (correct: per-channel keystore isolation). Documented the new pattern in `README.md` under "Per-channel builds" with the suffix-to-launcher-label mapping. iOS variants still need an `expo prebuild --platform ios --clean` to land bundle-id changes in `ios/<scheme>.xcodeproj` — not needed for current Android testing, noted in `app.config.js` inline comments and the README for when iOS internal builds come up. The current preview APK Mohammed has (a087fabb, hardcoded `in.deen.app`) is now superseded — new preview build 2515c08f produces `in.deen.app.preview` which installs alongside Play Store production with no uninstall needed.

worklog.title: Strategy + engineering uplift (PARTS 1–6)
worklog.summary: Commits: 587f793 (gitignore + LF enforcement), 49a1374 (release.yml graceful skip), 89a7a9f (v1.7.x plan docs + FIELD_NOTES), 81d4076 (1.7.0 release-notes tightened + locales/screenshots/banner), 375261a (ios widget entitlements + marketing site source), 0fcc0d5 (bug-solving playbook + Claude dev ecosystem brief), 6efa3ea (/ship slash command + tsc hook + INSTALL.md), e741961 (Q3-2026 strategy memo) Six-part pre-push and engineering uplift after the v1.7.3 hotfix landed. Cleaned the working tree (committed FIELD_NOTES, plan docs, store-listing tightening, site/, ios/ widget entitlements; gitignored 28MB of design assets + HLS chunks; added `.gitattributes` for LF-only normalization across Windows/Linux to stop EAS hash drift). Fixed `.github/workflows/release.yml` — replaced step-level `if: env.X` guards (which never worked because env was step-local) with a preflight that validates every credential end-to-end (presence + valid base64 + plausible content), writes ANDROID_READY/IOS_READY to GITHUB_ENV, and gates each downstream step. First green release-workflow run in project history (graceful-skipped both platforms with clear `::warning::` log messages instead of going red). Wrote two long engineering docs — `docs/engineering/bug-solving-playbook.md` (intake card format, severity ladder, Sentry→GH auto-ingest wiring, repro harness ladder, the iron 3-failed-fixes architecture-review rule, 5-min post-mortem template) and `docs/engineering/claude-dev-ecosystem.md` (3,800 words: install/evaluate/skip across plugins, MCPs, OSS agentic tooling, marketplaces, best-practice posts, with cost-routing advice — Sonnet default / Opus arch-only / Haiku one-shots → realistic 50-70% bill cut). Wired two concrete Claude Code additions: a `/ship` slash command at `.claude/commands/ship.md` that codifies the bump → CHANGELOG → 8-locale release notes → tag → push → workflow-watch dance (saves ~30 min per release), and a `PostToolUse` hook at `.claude/hooks/typecheck-after-edit.mjs` that runs `tsc --noEmit` after TS/TSX edits and feeds relevant errors back via additionalContext (smoke-tested clean + skip paths). Plus `.claude/INSTALL.md` listing the plugin checklist for the rest. Closed with `docs/strategy/Q3-2026-thinking.md` — 290-line memo ranking the next three product moves: iOS App Store launch (1, 4-6 days, biggest multiplier), Hifz mode with SM-2 spaced repetition (2, 3 weeks, defensible differentiator), "Support deen.in" patron flow (3, 1 week, story multiplier not revenue play). Includes alternatives considered and rejected, sequencing through Q3, and honest gaps a real team would close that we won't.

worklog.title: v1.7.3 audio control hotfix
worklog.summary: Two user-reported audio bugs from device-testing of v1.7.2 patched within the session and shipped as v1.7.3. Per-ayah pause button on `app/quran/[surah].tsx` was unconditionally calling `loadAndPlay` regardless of state — tapping pause silently restarted the verse from the top instead of pausing. Replaced with a three-branch toggle: same verse + playing → `pauseAudio`; same verse + paused but loaded → `playAudio`; otherwise → `loadAndPlay`. Required adding `isPlaying`, `pauseAudio`, `playAudio` selectors to the existing `useAudioStore` slice. Word-by-word tap audio race: rapid taps were starting overlapping `createAudioPlayer` flows, and whichever finished loading last would win — sometimes that wasn't the word the user last tapped. Added a `wordPlayGenRef` generation counter (`useRef(0)`) with three guard checks (after createAudioPlayer, in `onPlaybackStatusUpdate` listener, before `play()`) so any stale call from a previous tap releases its player and bails. Bumped versionCode 57→58 / iOS buildNumber 19→20. Wrote 8 locale release notes (en-US, en-GB, ar, hi-IN, ml-IN, ms, tr-TR, ur) plus combined.txt, all verified under Play Console's 500-codepoint cap. Tagged v1.7.3 and pushed; release workflow ran green for the first time.

worklog.title: Notification reliability overhaul (Batch 1 + 2)
worklog.summary: Foundation pass: USE_EXACT_ALARM permission (Android 14+ default-deny fix), stable per-prayer-per-day identifiers (`adhan_${prayer}_${YYYY-MM-DD}`), idempotent diff-based scheduling instead of cancel-all-then-recreate, default `nudgeEnabled: false` (most-reported "duplicate adhan" cause), removed iOS foreground double-playback, singleton listener guard, unified Test button. Hardening pass: self-heal listener on AppState 'active' that re-runs scheduling when scheduled count drops below threshold (catches OEM kills on Xiaomi/Samsung/Realme), versioned per-prayer channels (`adhan-fajr-v2`, etc.) with auto-bump on sound change, permission-revoked detection with `notificationsBlocked` / `timeSensitiveBlocked` flags, new Notification Health screen at /settings/notification-health (count, next 5, permissions, re-schedule action, OS settings deep link).

worklog.title: PostHog product-feature event capture
worklog.summary: Previously only lifecycle events flowed (Application Opened/Backgrounded). Now captures: `quran_played` (surah, ayah, reciter, mode), `prayer_marked_done` (prayer, on_time — best-effort via cachedPrayerTimes within ±6h), `bookmark_added` (verse + page variants), `translation_downloaded` (edition, verse_count), `surah_opened`, `mushaf_opened`, `feature_opened` (home tools grid), `adhan_notification_opened` (via singleton response listener filtered on data.type='adhan'). All gated on null-safe wrapper; non-throwing.

worklog.title: Mushaf layout migration to verified Madani source
worklog.summary: User-reported "many ayah ending mistakes" was a layout bug, not a text bug. Old generator used quran.com word-level `line_number` calibrated for QPC glyph fonts — when rendered as plain Uthmani text, end-of-verse markers (numerals) appeared on the line AFTER where the verse actually finishes. Sample of 30 random pages: 15/30 had at least one misplaced marker, 60 total mismatched lines. Wrote new generator using King Fahd Madani layout reference (zonetecde/mushaf-layout) for line/page boundaries, kept quran.com `text_uthmani` for verse text (text itself was correct). Regenerated `assets/data/mushaf-pages.json`: 0/30 mismatches after fix. Schema unchanged — fully backward-compatible. Added `npm run generate:mushaf:tanzil` script.

worklog.title: Sentry production-error guards
worklog.summary: Two top Sentry issues (8 events / 6 users + 6 events / 4 users in 24h) traced to missing try/catch: 1. `ExpoLocation.removeWatchAsync rejected` in qibla screen cleanup — wrapped each `.remove()` call individually so failure on one doesn't prevent the other; logs via `console.warn`, doesn't throw from cleanup. 2. `NativeDatabase.prepareSync rejected` in offline translation manager — wrapped all 5 sync DB calls (runSync, getFirstSync, getAllSync) with sensible fallbacks: `false` for isDownloaded checks, `[]` for getDownloadedEditions, `0` for verse count, structured throws for downloadTranslation so callers can surface to user. 3. Module.swift `updateActivity` and `endActivity` converted to `AsyncFunction` (matching `startActivity` from earlier commit) — eliminates fire-and-forget pattern where JS got "ended" before ActivityKit work completed.

worklog.title: Word-by-word Quran audio synchronisation
worklog.summary: Previously highlighting was tap-driven only — no connection to audio playback. Now: when audio is playing the displayed ayah AND the reciter is in the QDC-supported set (Mishary 7, Sudais 2, Abdul Basit 1, Husary 5), fetch per-word timing data from `api.qurancdn.com/api/qdc/audio/reciters/{id}/audio_files` (audio_segments tuples), increase `expo-audio` `updateInterval` from default 500ms to 100ms, and drive `activeWordIndex` from current playback position. Bounded-LRU cache (50 entries), Hermes-safe AbortController + setTimeout (replaced `AbortSignal.timeout` which crashes on Hermes), full-surah mode gated to prevent stale per-ayah timings during full-surah playback.

worklog.title: iOS widget bridge + Live Activity race fix + design parity
worklog.summary: Bridge: `checkWidgetBridgeAvailable()` exposed for early-return guards, `__DEV__` console.warn when native module is null (was silent no-op). Live Activity `startActivity` converted from `Function` + `Task { }` (race: end-old + request-new fired in parallel, ActivityKit's 1-activity-per-app limit silently rejected the new request) to `AsyncFunction` with sequential `await` of all `activity.end()` before `Activity.request()`. Design pass: 1px white@0.12 ContainerRelativeShape stroke on NextPrayer + PrayerTimes widgets, 3-stop deeper gradient (0/0.6/1.0), gold (#E2C98B) accent for secondary text (countdown timer, location), Live Activity lock screen wrapped in ZStack with gradient bg + RoundedRectangle 16pt border.

worklog.title: Quran screen performance pass
worklog.summary: FlatList perf: extracted `SurahListItem` as top-level `memo()` component, `renderItem` and handlers wrapped in `useCallback`, `ListHeaderComponent` JSX moved into `useMemo`, fixed `directAyahMatch` raw-const dep that busted `filteredSurahs` memo every render. `[surah].tsx`: `loadVerses` useCallback with correct deps, `renderItem` useCallback, `ListFooterComponent` useMemo, all bookmark handlers memoized. `mushaf.tsx`: `pageTitle`, `currentVerseKey`, `gridSlots`, `panResponder` useMemo. Then second pass: extracted 5 inline style array literals from inside `SurahListItem` body to defeat shallow-comparison bypass; removed unused `width` dep from `panResponder` deps array. Final pass: `[surah].tsx` switched from full-store destructure to 5 sliced `useAudioStore(s => ...)` selectors so the screen doesn't re-render 10×/sec under Task 9's faster tick rate.

worklog.title: Reciter URL audit (full-surah mode)
worklog.summary: Live-tested all reciter URLs against quranicaudio.com/qdc CDN. 5 wrong slugs corrected (Husary murattal, Husary muallim, Minshawy murattal, Abu Bakr Shatri, Yasser Ad-Dussary). 15 reciters had no QDC entry — `fullSurahSlug` removed from those entries; they fall back cleanly to per-ayah everyayah.com (all per-ayah URLs tested clean). All 25 reciters preserved in the array.

worklog.title: iPhone haptic feedback throughout app
worklog.summary: expo-haptics added to: play/pause buttons (Medium impact), surah navigation (Light), bookmark add (Success notification), prayer mark-done (Success), prayer un-mark (Light), Mushaf page nav (Light), Mushaf bookmark add (Success). No haptic on tap-only interactions to avoid noise.

worklog.title: iOS time-sensitive prayer notifications
worklog.summary: Added `interruptionLevel: 'timeSensitive'` to both adhan + nudge notification content (iOS 15+ — bypasses Focus mode for users who grant Time Sensitive permission). Explicit iOS permission flags in `requestPermissionsAsync`: `{ ios: { allowAlert: true, allowSound: true, allowBadge: true } }`. Schedule horizon extended from "today only" to 7 days × 5 prayers = 35 notifications max (well under iOS 64-limit). Then removed invalid option `allowDisplayInNotificationCenter` (not part of expo-notifications iOS permission shape).

worklog.title: Multi-surah Mushaf page audio continuation
worklog.summary: When a Mushaf page contains two surahs (one ending mid-page, the next starting), pressing play would play through the first surah's verses then stop dead — the audio system had no way to know what came next. Fix: `mushaf.tsx togglePlayPause` now builds a `pageQueue` from `page.verses` (surahNumber + ayahNumber pairs) and passes it as a 6th arg to `audioStore.loadAndPlay`. The store carries the queue; on per-ayah finish, it consults the queue for the next entry instead of incrementing within the current surah.

worklog.title: Audio pause race condition + ayah-by-ayah fix
worklog.summary: Pause was being ignored when tapped during async audio load (player.play() would fire after pause completed). Fix: module-level `pauseRequested` flag, set true on every pause(), checked just before `player.play()` is called inside loadAndPlay; if pauseRequested is true, skip play() entirely. `set({ isPlaying: false })` moved outside `if (player)` guard so isPlaying tracks user intent regardless of player state. AppState subscription stored as `_appStateSub` to prevent leaks; `stop()` clears `pageQueue = null`. Ayah-by-ayah play/pause unblocked by same flag mechanism.

worklog.title: Operations dashboard scaffolded
worklog.summary: Built the .dashboard/ refresher (Node stdlib, no deps), GitHub Actions workflow, public deeni-dashboard scaffold, password-gated Linear-style HTML, alert engine, and STATUS.md generator. Plan in DASHBOARD_PLAN.md. Awaiting first run after Phase 0 token setup.

worklog.title: iOS widgets premium redesign
worklog.summary: Live ticking countdown via Text(timerInterval:countsDown:), accent-rail next-prayer highlight on the Times widget, gold pull-quote treatment on Daily Dua, oversized quote-glyph backdrop on Daily Hadith. iOS 18 tinted-mode safe via widgetAccentable + widgetRenderingMode environment.

worklog.title: iOS Next Prayer widget stuck on "in 0:00"
worklog.summary: Root cause: src/widgets/syncWidgetData.tsx returned early on iOS so App Group group.in.deen.app was never written. Widget fell back to placeholder sample, system overlaid stale-data spinner. Fix: bridge through requireNativeModule('ReactNativeWidgetExtension')'s setString + reloadAllTimelines, wire 7-day schedule sync via syncPrayerScheduleToWidget.

worklog.title: DB + OTA hardening
worklog.summary: WAL journal_mode + foreign_keys=ON pragmas on every open; AppState listener that re-opens the SQLite handle on Android foreground after potential TRIM_MEMORY invalidation; runtimeVersion switched from hardcoded "1.3.4" to { policy: "fingerprint" }; Sentry tags for app.version / platform / build.number; bootstrap captureException; DB breadcrumbs in offline.ts.

worklog.title: v1.4.0 bump + expo-audio migration
worklog.summary: Migrated adhanPlayer + ruqyah screen from deprecated expo-av to expo-audio. Wrapped void-returning setActiveForLockScreen / clearLockScreenControls in try/catch instead of .catch(). Added optional context/repetitions/quranRefs to RuqyahVerse. bump-version.mjs now keeps semver, versionCode, buildNumber, and package.json synced in lockstep.
